description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular, single-story agricultural building with a wide, gently sloped gable roof covered in reddish-brown tiles. The structure features wooden plank siding in a natural finish, with several rectangular windows evenly spaced along the longer sides, providing natural light and ventilation. The roof extends outward to form protective overhangs on both long sides, supported by simple posts on one side, creating a sheltered area for livestock or equipment. The main entrance appears to be a large, sliding barn door, centrally located on the front facade, flanked by windows. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing function and durability, with minimal decorative elements. The building sits elevated slightly above ground level, likely to prevent water ingress and improve airflow.
description: A rectangular wooden cowshed with a wide, reddish-brown gable roof, multiple side windows, and a large central sliding barn door. The roof overhangs are supported by posts, creating sheltered exterior areas.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide gable roof, main sliding barn door, and supporting posts under the roof overhang.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long, sloped roof and the building's narrow profile, with posts visible at the front.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full length of the cowshed, multiple windows, and the extended roof overhang without visible doors.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the narrow end of the building and the continuation of the gable roof.
attribute_door_style: sliding barn door, wooden construction with metal hardware
attribute_window_presence: four rectangular windows on the long sides, evenly spaced
